topic: Two coworkers discover they went to the same summer camp
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office seemed to hum with an irritating frequency that matched the rising tension in Clara’s chest. She meticulously stacked the quarterly reports, her movements sharp and precise, every crease in the paper a testament to her frustration. Then, across the aisle, she saw him. Mark. He was leaning back in his chair, a slow, almost lazy smile playing on his lips as he scrolled through his phone. A jolt, sharp and unwelcome, shot through her. He looked exactly like someone she hadn't seen in years—a slightly sun-kissed complexion, a familiar slouch. Her jaw tightened, and she deliberately picked up a pen, clicking it rapidly against the desk. *Impossible,* she thought, the word tasting like ash. She needed a distraction, anything to push the unwelcome memory of that distant summer camp out of her mind.

[story 2]

"Seriously?" Liam sighed, running a hand through his already messy hair. He stared at the shared photo on the company intranet—a grainy picture of a lake surrounded by towering pines. His brow furrowed deep enough to cast shadows across his eyes. He glanced over at Sarah, who was currently tapping her foot with an almost aggressive rhythm under the desk. Liam felt a distinct tightening in his shoulders, a knot forming just beneath his ribs. He let out a short, sharp exhale, the sound laced with disbelief. "No way," he muttered, shaking his head slowly. He looked around the open-plan office, suddenly hyper-aware of the shared space, the proximity amplifying the sudden wave of irritation washing over him. He needed coffee, black and scalding, something to reset the static buzzing inside his head.

[story 3]

"You actually went to Camp Whispering Pines?" Maya asked, her voice dangerously level as she reviewed the shared travel itinerary. She didn't look up from her screen, but the way she gripped her mouse suggested a significant internal upheaval. A faint tremor ran through her hand. She let out a short, dismissive puff of air, focusing intently on the spreadsheet, refusing to acknowledge the coincidence. *Of course,* she thought, a bitter taste rising in her throat. She deliberately straightened her posture, pulling her cardigan tighter around herself, wishing the entire shared history between them would simply evaporate. The silence she cultivated around herself was thick and brittle.
